Decide what to block


Choose any additional category to block

Tradeoffs

Regexes to automatically trust or block

Trust pages using their url

You can deactivate the automatic blocker on some pages by specifying patterns that would be matched to their urls ( more info on regex ). Blocking images using regexes is also deactivated. Manual blocking is sill possible by right-clicking images.

For instance ^https?://en\.wikipedia\.org/wiki/Tibetan_Mastiff means that the automatic blocker will be deactivated when visiting the page https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tibetan_Mastiff.

One regex per line is expected.

Block images using their src

You can automatically block some images by specifying a pattern that would be matched to their src.

For instance example\.com/scarySpiderImages/.* means that images with src like https://example.com/scarySpiderImages/spider.jpg are immediately blocked without being analysed by the automatic blocker.

Unblock images using their src

You can automatically trust some images by specifying a pattern that would be matched to their src. Blocking regexes have priority.

For instance example\.com/TibetanMastiff/.* means that images with src like https://example.com/TibetanMastiff/puppy.jpg are immediately unblocked without being analysed by the automatic blocker.

Miscellaneous

Description

PhobiaBlocker blocks scary images of your phobia.

Features

  • Automatically block images representing physical things you chose
  • Manually block or unblock images with a right-click
  • Block all images on all websites
  • Automatically block or trust (for performance saving) images whose src matches a given regex (blocking has priority)
  • Add trusted pages (for performance saving) whose url matches a given regex

Options

  • Quick selection to block spider-like and insect-like images
  • Advanced selection from a long list of physical things
  • Speed/Accuracy tradeoff of the automatic blocker
  • Risk management of the automatic blocker
  • Add or remove a regex to automatically trust pages using their url, trust or block images using their src
  • Label trusted websites from the popup
  • Pause the automatic blocker (manual blocking is still enabled)
  • Block all images on all websites without exception
  • Enable dark or light mode (dark mode by default)

Privacy

  • No browsing data is sent to an external endpoint
  • Images are analysed on your computer but they are not saved in any way except after transformation in a cache that can be disabled in the options

Technical details and restrictions

  • Machine Learning models for imagenet classification are used to analyse images. The list of things to block comes from imagenet classes. This is why only physical things are available. For instance blocking abstract phobia like fear of heights or gore is not supported.
  • Are analysed <img> and svg elements <image>. When a new image is added to the DOM or is changing, the element is also analysed. Images from canvas and css property background-image are not supported.
  • Images too small to be analysed are ignored.
  • Images are first temporary blocked before they are analysed. Images can blink before being temporary blocked in very rare cases.
  • Around 99% of the size of the extension comes from the Machine Learning models and the library to execute them.
  • A cache of the automatic blocker decisions is used and is persisted locally. The cache contains non cryptographic hashes of images and takes at most less than 1MB. Both the cache and its persistence can be disabled in the options.

As a bonus a little surprise for Tibetan Mastiff lovers! (Can be disabled in the options)
